violations.text: The presence of small rodents is not controlled as evidenced by droppings observed in warewashing/ prep area of kitchen on the floor. The presence of insects, rodents, and other pests must be controlled by routinely inspecting the premises for evidence of pests, using acceptable methods to control pests, eliminating harborage conditions, and routinely inspecting incoming shipments of food and supplies. Establishment to continue with exclusion processes and work in conjunction with pest control services. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 01/23/2026

The presence of small rodents is not controlled as evidenced by droppings observed in warewashing/ prep area of kitchen. The presence of insects, rodents, and other pests must be controlled by routinely inspecting the premises for evidence of pests, using acceptable methods to control pests, eliminating harborage conditions, and routinely inspecting incoming shipments of food and supplies. Establishment to continue with exclusion processes and work in conjunction with pest control services. New Violation. Correct By: 01/12/2026
The non-food contact surfaces of the various storage shelving and fanguards in produce walk-in cooler have an accumulation white residue and dust. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 01/12/2026

Establishment's Rhode Island Manager Certified In Food Safety licenses are expired. Establishment has multiple individuals with manager food safety certificate. FMC to re-instate or individual to register. New Violation. Correct By: 01/29/2026

An open employees' beverage container was observed stored in a food preparation area (in kitchen and at bar). Employees shall not drink from open beverage containers in order to prevent contamination of their hands, the container, and exposed food. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 12/29/2025

Employee was observed rinsing pan in handwashing sink on the kitchen line. A hand-washing sink must not be used for purposes other than hand washing. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 12/29/2025

Food contact surfaces of the ice machine were observed with an accumulation of pink and black residue. The food contact surfaces of equipment must be clean to sight and touch. Ice was voluntarily disposed of. Ice machine was actively being cleaned and sanitized during time of inspection. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 12/29/2025

The doors to the outside (guest entrance and kitchen entrance) are not tight-fitting. Outer openings of a food establishment must be protected against the entry of insects and rodents by tight-fitting doors. New Violation. Correct By: 01/12/2026

The presence of small rodents is not controlled as evidenced by droppings observed in warewashing/ prep area of kitchen. The presence of insects, rodents, and other pests must be controlled by routinely inspecting the premises for evidence of pests, using acceptable methods to control pests, eliminating harborage conditions, and routinely inspecting incoming shipments of food and supplies. New Violation. Correct By: 01/12/2026

Single service items are not being stored properly in their original protective package and/or are stored on a surface that is not clean. Cardboard pizza boxes were observed in direct contact with rodent droppings. Single service items were voluntarily disposed of. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 12/29/2025

Wood is used in the construction of sink mounting in the kitchen area. Non-food contact surfaces of equipment that are exposed to splash, spillage, or other food soiling or that require frequent cleaning must be constructed of a nonabsorbent and smooth material. New Violation. Correct By: 01/29/2026

The non-food contact surfaces of the various storage shelving in warewashing/prep area and produce walk-in cooler have an accumulation of frayed foil, dust, dirt, food residue, and/or rodent droppings. New Violation. Correct By: 12/29/2025
Stagnant water was observed by produce/dairy cooler. The physical facilities shall be cleaned as often as necessary to keep them clean. New Violation. Correct By: 01/12/2026

Shellfish tags must be maintained for 90 days in chronological order and the date when the last shellfish was used from the bag must be noted on the shellfish tag. PIC informed. New Violation. Correct By: 01/29/2025

Food contact surfaces of the slicer were observed with an accumulation of food and debris. The food contact surfaces of equipment must be clean to sight and touch. Slicer was cleaned and sanitized during time of inspection. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 01/29/2025
Remoulade sauce was observed held past 7 days in the walk-in cooler. Food was voluntarily disposed of. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 01/29/2025
The consumer advisory provided does not disclose all the foods on the menu that are raw or undercooked. PIC informed. New Violation. Correct By: 02/26/2025
